data.title: Woodlands Drive 17 EC site to draw 3–6 bidders
data.description:
Pricing will likely reference the adjacent EC plot awarded in August 2025.

Moderate to healthy bidding interest is expected for the newly launched executive condominium (EC) site at Woodlands Drive 17, with all three major brokers—PropNex, Huttons, and Realion—forecasting between three and six bidders.

Top bids are projected in the range of $700 to $800 per square foot per plot ratio (psf ppr), reflecting both developer confidence and pricing discipline in the EC land market.

EC tenders are seen as broadly stable, and CDL—winner of the adjacent site awarded earlier in 2025—is considered a likely participant in this round.

Launched under the 2H2025 Government Land Sales (GLS) programme, the site is expected to yield around 560 units. Realion notes the tender will close on 13 January 2026.

Affordability constraints remain a key consideration, with EC buyers subject to a 30% Mortgage Servicing Ratio (MSR) and a household income ceiling of $16,000.

These structural limits cap developers’ pricing power, even as recent EC launches have demonstrated strong buyer demand. Realion highlighted Aurelle of Tampines, which sold 90% of its units during its launch weekend and was fully sold within a month.

Pricing will likely reference the adjacent EC plot awarded in August 2025, which saw five bids and set a new record of $782 psf ppr, above the previous high of $768 psf ppr at Tampines Street 95.

Supply scarcity adds further context. Woodlands has not seen new EC stock in nearly a decade. The last GLS EC site in the planning area was Northwave in 2015, and Bellewoods was launched in 2013.

Brokers said this gap may contribute to pent-up demand, particularly given the large upgrader base—around 6,500 HDB flats in the area were completed between 2016 and 2018.

The site also benefits from location fundamentals. It is within walking distance of Woodlands South MRT on the Thomson-East Coast Line, offering direct links to Orchard and the CBD, and convenient access to Changi Airport.

It is also one stop from Woodlands Regional Centre and two stops from the upcoming RTS Link to Johor Bahru, scheduled to begin operations in 2027.

Amenities in the area include Woodlands Health Campus and several primary schools. Huttons estimates up to three within a 1km radius, whilst Realion names Innova and Woodgrove Primary specifically.

Brokers noted continued transformation of Woodlands Regional Centre and growing cross-border connectivity could strengthen long-term demand.

data.title: Singapore logistics market holds steady with 3PL-led demand
data.description:
Year-to-date, net absorption reached 1.9 million square feet against 5.8 million square feet of completions

Singapore’s logistics and warehouse property market saw steady leasing momentum in Q3 2025, with leasing enquiries rising quarter-on-quarter.

According to JLL, demand was led by third-party logistics providers (3PLs) and end-users seeking large ambient spaces of 100,000 square feet or more, as well as chiller and freezer units between 40,000 and 120,000 square feet, mainly for wine and food storage.

Notable tenant movements included DSV’s opening of its Red Lion2 facility in July and SEKO Logistics’ relocation to 50 Alps Avenue, taking up 8,000 square feet.

On the supply side, new completions slowed during the quarter. Key additions included the second phase of World Gateway 2, a redevelopment at 5 Toh Guan Road East by CapitaLand Ascendas REIT (CLAR) completed ahead of schedule, and an addition/alteration at Toll City (60 Pioneer Road).

In future pipeline activity, KWE Singapore broke ground on a redevelopment project at 20 Changi South Avenue 2, which will include air-conditioned mezzanine space for temperature-controlled cargo and is scheduled for completion in 2027.

Rental performance remained flat. Islandwide average rents held steady for the fifth consecutive quarter at SGD 1.71 per square foot per month. Capital values and yields also remained unchanged during the same period.

Year-to-date, net absorption reached 1.9 million square feet against 5.8 million square feet of completions, whilst the overall vacancy rate stood at 10.9%. JLL categorized the rental cycle as stable, with zero year-on-year rent growth.

Looking ahead, no new completions for lease are expected in the next 12 months, which may ease pressure on vacancy rates.

However, JLL cautioned that global economic uncertainties could limit near-term demand and cap rental growth over the next six months.

With borrowing costs stabilizing and investor interest holding, yields are expected to remain steady, whilst capital value appreciation is likely to stay muted in the short term due to the flat rental outlook.

data.title: CBD leasing demand holds firm in Q3 with AI and fintech expansion
data.description:
The vacancy rate held at 7.0%, whilst average gross effective rents stood at $11.83 per square foot per month.

Leasing demand in Singapore’s CBD office market remained resilient in Q3 2025, supported by continued expansion from sectors such as artificial intelligence and digital finance.

According to JLL, companies like Ripple, Jane Street, and Zoom contributed to healthy leasing activity during the quarter.

No new office completions were recorded in the quarter, and the forward supply pipeline remains limited.

Only a few projects—Shaw Tower, Solitaire on Cecil, and Newport Tower—are expected to be completed through 2027. In addition, 39 Robinson is set to re-enter the market in late 2026 after undergoing refurbishment.

CBD gross effective rents saw their fastest growth in six quarters, although this was largely attributed to the inclusion of the new IOI Central Boulevard Towers in the rental basket.

Excluding that addition, rents continued their steady trend, registering sub-1% growth for the sixth consecutive quarter. Capital values moved in tandem with rents, whilst yields remained stable.

Market fundamentals in the CBD showed year-to-date net absorption of 0.3 million square feet against 0.6 million square feet of new supply.

The vacancy rate held at 7.0%, whilst average gross effective rents stood at $11.83 per square foot per month. On a year-on-year basis, rents rose 2.9%, and the overall rental cycle was assessed as stable.

Looking ahead, JLL noted that persistent trade and geopolitical uncertainties are likely to keep expansion and relocation activity cautious for the remainder of 2025.

With IOI Central Boulevard Towers nearing full occupancy, whole-floor and multi-floor space options in high-quality buildings are becoming increasingly scarce. This tightening supply could accelerate both rental and capital value growth in 2026.

data.title: Daily Markets Briefing: STI down 0.28%; Top stock is Yangzijiang Shipbuilding
data.description:
Yangzijiang Shipbuilding is the top stock with a 1.88% increase.

The Straits Times Index (STI) closed at 4,356.200, down 0.28% on Thursday, 16 October, at 5:21 p.m.

Yangzijiang Shipbuilding was the most active stock, followed by Keppel (+1.51%) and Venture (+1.39%).

SGX and HongkongLand USD were at the foot.

data.title: IOI Properties to launch W Residences Marina View for public sale
data.description:
Public sale will open on 25 October. 

IOI Properties Singapore will launch its W Residences Marina View - Singapore, the first hotel-home integrated residene,  for public sale on 25 October.

The public launch will be a limited release of 100 units, out of the planned 683 units, from levels 16 to 20. Out of the 100 units available, 85 will be the one-, two-, and three-bedroom residences, starting from $3,230 per square foot.

Complementing these are 15 homes with 4 and 5 bedrooms in the Signature Collection.

W Residences Marina View residents are promised hotel services such as 24/7 concierge services, valet parking, and fixtures and appliances from V-ZUG, DornBracht, and TOTO, amongst others.

The residences are developed by IOI Properties, managed by Marriott International, and are designed by firm architects61 and interior designer Fady Hachem.

data.title: Soon Hock Enterprise to debut on SGX Mainboard
data.description:
The offering was 9.8 times oversubscribed.

Soon Hock Enterprise Holding Limited is set to debut on the SGX-ST Mainboard following strong demand for its Initial Public Offering (IPO).

The company is offering 21.58 million shares at S$0.58 each.

The offering was 9.8 times oversubscribed, with total interest and application monies reaching approximately $122.6m.

Specifically, the International Offer (18.78 million shares) received indications of interest totaling approximately $95.1m, making it 8.7 times oversubscribed.

The Singapore Public Offer (2.8 millio shares) was even more popular, being 16.9 times oversubscribed based on 1,444 valid applications for 47.33 million shares, totaling about $27.4m.

Maybank Securities and United Overseas Bank Limited are serving as the Joint Issue Managers, Joint Global Coordinators, and Joint Bookrunners for the IPO.

data.title: Q&M Dental eyes Thailand expansion with local dental chain deal
data.description:
The deal values the Thai company at $77.8m.

Q&M Dental Group (Singapore) plans to enter Thailand’s private dental market by acquiring an equity stake in a Thai dental clinic chain, according to a bourse filing.

The deal, outlined in a memorandum of understanding, values the Thai company at $77.8m (฿1.95b) on a cash- and debt-free basis, with final adjustments to be made upon completion.

The unnamed Thai target runs one of the country’s largest privately owned dental networks, with over 30 clinics across Thailand, mainly in Bangkok and the northeastern provinces of Udon Thani, Khon Kaen, and Ubon Ratchathani.

The acquisition would mark Q&M’s first move into Thailand, expanding its presence beyond Singapore and Malaysia.

Following the deal, one of the target company’s founders will sign a 15-year service agreement to remain as CEO or managing director.

The agreement also includes a profit guarantee that starts at $4.9m (฿123m) in 2026, rising to $11.4m (฿285m) by 2030, and remaining at least that level until 2033.

($1 = ฿25.08)

data.title: Jardine Cycle & Carriage appoints new independent chairman of the board
data.description:
Samuel Tsien will be independent chairman of the board, effective 11 November.

Jardine Cycle & Carriage has appointed Samuel Tsien as its independent chairman of the board, effective 11 November.

He is currently a non-executive independent director and was first appointed to the board in October 2021.

Prior to this, he was Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation’s group CEO from 2021 to 2022.

data.title: Gov't to curb F1 disruption on businesses
data.description:
This will include improving access and wayfinding around Marina Bay.

Singapore government agencies will implement measures to reduce disruption on businesses during the F1 Singapore Grand Prix.

This will include improving access and wayfinding around Marina Bay and ensuring malls remain accessible by public transport and hired cars.

The road closure period has been significantly reduced, dropping to seven days today from 12 days in 2008.

Since 2008, the grand prix has attracted over 720,000 international visitors and generated more than $2.2b in incremental tourism receipts.

data.title: OxPay utilises net proceeds from convertible loan of $1.8m
data.description:
It has been used for operating expenses, amongst others.

OxPay Financial Limited has fully utilised net proceeds from convertible loan of $1.8m, after deducting the costs and expenses of approximately $161,000.

It has been used for operating expenses, employee remuneration, repayment of existing loan facilities, and directors’ fees.

The company entered into a loan agreement with Oxley Capital Management last 17 January 2025 to which the lender granted up to $2m.
